title: Empirical Fact
description: The text claims objective, external reality. It relies entirely on direct, observable physics, testable mechanics, or strict mathematical proof

title: Philosophical Assertion
description: The text asserts a logical, ethical, or aesthetic truth relying entirely on reason, intuition, and debate rather than physical evidence

title: Personal Reality
description: The text anchors itself to personal experience and context, but exclusively claims the truth of the author's internal, biased, emotional experience

title: Consensus Reality
description: The text anchors itself to shared historical authority and documented cultural agreement, relying on inherited trust rather than direct physical observation

title: Synthesized Reality
description: The text is anchored in Empirical, Philosophical, Personal, or Consensus reality, but actively introduces fictional elements, characters, or scenarios to construct a synthesized account

title: Absolute Fiction
description: The text is definitively not true in base reality. It is a complete fabrication built to explore its own internal universe and mechanics

title: Averacit
description: The text is entirely divorced from the concept of truth. It constructs no reality and exists purely as an abstract, aesthetic, or structural mechanism of language
